View Text: Former fossil fuels executive Gwyn Morgan has been quietly backing a network of Canadian think tanks, right wing Facebook pages and media organizations disputing the climate crisis.

The Gwyn Morgan and Patricia Trottier Foundation has donated millions of dollars to Canadian think tanks affiliated with the Atlas Network, a powerful international organization that counts 500 "free market", anti-regulation member groups around the world. Atlas's international network creates and promotes materials downplaying climate change and the need for climate policy, advocating for corporate-friendly policies while accepting donations from large international corporations.

View Title: Member of Original Atlas Network Think Tank
View Text: Morgan has been a member of the first Atlas Network group, the Fraser Institute, as recently as 2023. Fraser advocates against climate regulations while receiving money from oil companies like Encana/Ovintiv (founded by Morgan) and Imperial Oil's parent company ExxonMobil, as well as right wing American donor groups like the Koch, Scaife and Templeton Foundations.

DeSmog revealed that Morgan has donated to True North/Juno News, a far-right Canadian media organization that platforms hate groups and climate deniers. True North is run by former Alberta provincial conservative and Canada Strong and Free Network (formerly Manning Centre) staffers.

View Title: Longtime Supporter of Canada Strong and Free Network
View Text: Morgan has also been a large donor and partner to another key Canadian Atlas Network group, the conservative Canada Strong and Free Network (formerly the Manning Centre). CSFN platforms the same climate deniers and climate denial arguments as other Atlas Network groups, while being supported financially by large oil and gas companies.

View Title: Money Goes to "Grassroots" Conservative Campaign Advertising
View Text: Canada Strong and Free Network has given money to a similarly named group, Canada Strong and Proud, registered as a third-party-advertiser by the CFO of the Modern Miracle Network. MMN's CEO, Michael Binnion, is the chair of CSFN and has publicly asked oil and gas companies to fund CSFN through MMN. Canada Strong and Proud is connected to Jeff Ballingall and his Canada Proud organizations. The layers of similarly named organizations is likely meant to obfuscate that these so-called "grassroots" groups were paid for by rich individuals.

View Title: Advocates Right-Wing Views In Traditional Media
View Text: Morgan pushes his pro-industry, anti-regulation views in pieces in the National Post and Globe and Mail. His writing suggests his right wing outlook goes far beyond economic libertarianism. A recent Financial Post article featured the subtitle, "Ditching nuclear power in exchange for Russian gas was a big unforced policy error and so was essentially unlimited refugee immigration".

View Title: Joins with CEOs to Advertise for Pierre Poilievre
View Text: During the 2025 election, Morgan and 32 other powerful business leaders paid for ads in traditional Canadian media outlets advocating for Pierre Poilievre and the Conservative Party of Canada. Amongst the signers are CEOs for the most powerful oil companies in Canada.
